files.year_nibbles_format: uint8_t[30000001]; nibble[orderkey] = (year-1992) in lower nibble of byte[orderkey/2] for even orderkeys, upper nibble for odd; 0xF = invalid

storage_extensions.usage: mmap year_nibbles.bin with MADV_RANDOM. After mmapping, immediately call madvise(MADV_WILLNEED) to pre-fault pages into L3 cache during the dim_filter phase. Hot-path access: int year_off = (yn[lorderkey>>1] >> ((lorderkey&1)<<2)) & 0xF; if (year_off > 6) continue; — single byte access into 28.6MB L3-resident array. Replaces the two-level orders_by_orderkey(240MB) + o_orderdate(57MB) DRAM lookup chain, eliminating 2 DRAM misses per qualifying lineitem row.

join_order.notes: Read p_partkey.bin (8MB) and p_name.bin (112MB) via fread. Build two structures in one pass: (1) uint8_t green_part[2000001] — 2MB bool filter, L3-resident; (2) uint16_t partkey_to_compact[2000001] — 4MB mapping from partkey to compact index 0..N-1 (sentinel 0xFFFF = not green). Assign compact indices sequentially as green parts are discovered. Count total green parts N (expected ~110000). Single-threaded; on hot run p_name is in page cache so cost is ~1ms.

join_order.build: partsupp(ps_partkey, ps_suppkey, ps_supplycost) — compact flat array for green parts
join_order.notes: Runtime-built compact lookup: read ps_partkey.bin (30.5MB) + ps_suppkey.bin (30.5MB) + ps_supplycost.bin (61MB) — all in page cache on hot run, ~1.2ms total. For each of 8M partsupp rows: if partkey is green (green_part[ps_partkey[i]] != 0), store into compact_suppkeys[cidx][slot] and compact_costs[cidx][slot] where cidx = partkey_to_compact[ps_partkey[i]] and slot is a per-compact-idx counter (use int32_t slots_used[N] initialized to 0). In TPC-H each partkey has exactly 4 suppliers, so slot count stays within [4]. Resulting structures: int32_t compact_suppkeys[N][4] = ~1.76MB, double compact_costs[N][4] = ~3.52MB. Both L3-resident. Hot-path lookup: uint16_t cidx = p2c[lpartkey]; double sc = 0.0; for (int j=0;j<4;j++) { if (cs[cidx][j]==lsuppkey){sc=cc[cidx][j];break;} } — eliminates 256MB partsupp_hash_index DRAM access entirely.
Precompute int32_t suppkey_to_nationrow[100001] from supplier_by_suppkey + s_nationkey + nation_by_nationkey. 400KB, always L3-resident. Same as iter_0.

mmap year_nibbles.bin (28.6MB, existing). Issue MADV_WILLNEED immediately after mmap to pre-fault into L3 while dim_filter runs. Hot path: int year_off = (yn[lorderkey>>1] >> ((lorderkey&1)<<2)) & 0xF; if (year_off > 6) continue. Eliminates two-level 297MB DRAM lookup.
Load n_name (650 bytes). Fold into suppkey_to_nationrow precomputation.

data_structures.green_part_filter: uint8_t[2000001], 2MB, L3-resident; built during p_name scan
data_structures.partkey_to_compact: uint16_t[2000001], 4MB, L3-resident; sentinel 0xFFFF = not green; maps partkey to compact index 0..N-1 for green parts
data_structures.compact_suppkeys: int32_t[N][4] where N~110000, ~1.76MB, L3-resident; 4 suppkeys per green part (TPC-H invariant); built from ps_partkey+ps_suppkey
data_structures.compact_costs: double[N][4] where N~110000, ~3.52MB, L3-resident; 4 supplycosts per green part; built from ps_supplycost
data_structures.year_nibbles: mmap uint8_t[30000001], 28.6MB, L3-resident via MADV_WILLNEED; nibble[orderkey] = year-1992; access: (yn[key>>1]>>((key&1)<<2))&0xF
data_structures.aggregation: double profits[25][10] per thread in alignas(64) ThreadProfits struct — double, NOT long double; reduces false sharing and uses SSE2 FP units
data_structures.l3_hot_budget: green_part(2MB) + partkey_to_compact(4MB) + compact_suppkeys(1.76MB) + compact_costs(3.52MB) + year_nibbles(28.6MB) + suppkey_to_nationrow(0.4MB) = 40.3MB total, within 44MB LLC. No DRAM-exceeding random access on hot path.

parallelism.parallel_phase: lineitem scan (60M rows split into ~600 morsels across 64 threads)
parallelism.thread_local_state: double profits[25][10] in alignas(64) struct — avoids false sharing; MUST be double not long double
parallelism.reduction: single-threaded sum of per-thread profits[25][10] arrays after parallel phase

indexes.purpose: O(1) filter lineitem by l_partkey; 2MB L3-resident

indexes.index: partkey_to_compact uint16_t[2000001] + compact_suppkeys int32_t[N][4] + compact_costs double[N][4]
indexes.purpose: O(1) partsupp lookup: compact_idx = p2c[lpartkey]; scan 4 suppkeys for match; fetch cost. Total 9.28MB L3-resident, replaces 256MB partsupp_hash_index DRAM access.
O(1) l_orderkey -> year_offset 0-6; eliminates 2 DRAM misses per qualifying row vs two-level 297MB orders lookup

optimization_notes: Categories B + E. Root cause: (1) iter_3 REGRESSED because it used long double (80-bit x87) for profit accumulation — 3-4x slower than SSE2 double — negating the nibble optimization savings; (2) two structures remain DRAM-exceeding: orders chain (297MB, 6.7x LLC) and partsupp hash (256MB, 5.8x LLC). Strategy: use existing year_nibbles.bin (Category E) with MADV_WILLNEED pre-warming to eliminate the 2-DRAM-miss orders chain (28.6MB, L3-resident); build a compact partsupp flat array at runtime (Category B) — read ps_partkey/ps_suppkey/ps_supplycost (122MB total, ~1.2ms from page cache), produce partkey_to_compact uint16_t[2M]=4MB + compact_suppkeys int32_t[110K][4]=1.76MB + compact_costs double[110K][4]=3.52MB totaling 9.28MB — which replaces all 256MB partsupp_hash_index random access with direct L3-resident 4-entry linear scan. Combined L3 hot budget is 40.3MB, within the 44MB LLC. Iter_1 failed because its 16MB compact hash + 60MB orders array = 76MB exceeded LLC; this iteration's 40.3MB fits. The critical fix from iter_3 is using double (not long double) for profit accumulation.
